questions.question:
The four nucleotides in \( \text{DNA} \) are called \( \text{A, C, G} \), and \( \text{T} \). Consider the following languages over the alphabet \( \{\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{G} \), and \( \mathrm{T}\} \).
\[
\begin{array}{l}
L_{1}=\left\{(\mathrm{AC})^{n}(\mathrm{GT})^{n} \mid n \geq 0\right\} \\
L_{2}=\left\{\mathrm{A}^{n} \mathrm{C}^{n} \mathrm{G}^{n} \mathrm{~T}^{n} \mid n \geq 0\right\} \\
L_{3}=\left\{(\mathrm{AT})^{n}(\mathrm{CG})^{m} \mid m \neq n \text { and } m, n \geq 0\right\}
\end{array}
\]

Which of the above languages is/are context-free?



Theory of Computation: TIFR CSE 2024 | Part B | Question: 6
